title: viola tricolor subsp. subalpina
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. subalpina, also known as the small pansy or Alpine pansy, is a subspecies of the heartsease flower, a type of wild pansy that thrives in mountainous regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Viola tricolor subsp. subalpina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Viola tricolor subsp. subalpina include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
